name: Switch
description: A control that toggles a setting on or off, taking effect immediately. Built on Base UI Switch with StyleX styling.

Root.description: The switch track. Renders a <button role="switch"> with a hidden <input> beside it for form integration.

checked.description: Whether the switch is currently active. Use for controlled mode.

defaultChecked.description: Whether the switch is initially active. Use for uncontrolled mode.

onCheckedChange.description: Callback fired when the switch is activated or deactivated.

disabled.description: Whether the component should ignore user interaction.

readOnly.description: Whether the user should be unable to activate or deactivate the switch.

required.description: Whether the user must activate the switch before submitting a form.

name.description: Identifies the field when a form is submitted.

value.description: The value submitted with the form when the switch is on.

uncheckedValue.description: The value submitted with the form when the switch is off.

inputRef.description: A ref to access the hidden <input> element.

dataAttributes.data-checked: Present when the switch is on.
dataAttributes.data-unchecked: Present when the switch is off.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the switch is disabled.
dataAttributes.data-readonly: Present when the switch is read-only.
dataAttributes.data-required: Present when the switch is required.

Thumb.description: The movable part of the switch that slides between off and on positions.

dataAttributes.data-checked: Present when the switch is on.
dataAttributes.data-unchecked: Present when the switch is off.

cssRequirements.description: Reduced-motion support requires a global CSS rule. StyleX cannot target media queries on transitions across both root and thumb consistently, so consumers should add this rule for accessibility.

intents.reasoning: Switch is the standard control for binary settings that take effect immediately, like dark mode or notification toggles.

avoidWhen.scenario: Boolean option saved with a form (e.g., terms agreement)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Checkbox for boolean state that submits with a form rather than taking effect immediately.

avoidWhen.scenario: Single selection from a group of mutually exclusive options
avoidWhen.reasoning: Switch is for a single binary state. Use Radio for picking one option from many.
Switch represents a state, not an action. Use Button for actions.

examples.name: basic
examples.code:
<label style={{ display: 'flex', alignItems: 'center', gap: 8 }}>
  <Switch.Root>
    <Switch.Thumb />
  </Switch.Root>
  Enable notifications
</label>

examples.name: with-field
examples.description: Switch wrapped in a Field for label and description
